activities_projects.title: Deriving Heron’s Formula
activities_projects.objective: Understand and derive Heron’s formula using semi-perimeter
steps: Start with triangle ABC with sides a, b, c
Write semi-perimeter s = (a + b + c)/2
Use area formula √[s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)]
Verify derivation using one example triangle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands mathematical derivation of Heron’s formula

activities_projects.title: Area of Triangles with Large Decimal Sides
activities_projects.objective: Calculate areas of triangles with large decimal sides using Heron’s formula
steps: Take triangle sides a = 12.5 cm, b = 15.3 cm, c = 14.8 cm
Calculate semi-perimeter s
Use Heron’s formula to find area
Verify correctness by re-calculation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula for precise calculations

activities_projects.title: Comparing Areas of Multiple Triangles with Same Perimeter
activities_projects.objective: Explore which triangle has maximum area for given perimeter
steps: Choose perimeter P = 24 cm
Select multiple triangle side combinations with same P
Calculate areas using Heron’s formula
Compare areas to find maximum
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands effect of side distribution on area

activities_projects.title: Area of Triangles in Coordinate Plane
activities_projects.objective: Calculate area using Heron’s formula from coordinate distances
steps: Draw triangle with vertices A(x1,y1), B(x2,y2), C(x3,y3)
Calculate distances AB, BC, AC using distance formula
Compute semi-perimeter s
Use Heron’s formula to calculate area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula in coordinate geometry
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct distance calculation and area (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Project: Triangular Garden Design
activities_projects.objective: Design a triangular garden and calculate area for planting using Heron’s formula
steps: Choose dimensions for triangular garden plot
Draw triangle to scale on graph paper
Calculate sides if not measured
Use Heron’s formula to find area for planting plan
Label all sides and angles
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Integrates Heron’s formula into a real-life project
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct measurement, construction, and area calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Complex Triangle Area Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Compare areas of scalene, isosceles, and equilateral triangles with same perimeter
steps: Take three triangles with same perimeter but different types
Calculate semi-perimeter and area using Heron’s formula
Record and compare results
Discuss which triangle type maximizes area

activities_projects.title: Area of Triangles with Mixed Units
activities_projects.objective: Apply Heron’s formula to sides in meters, centimeters, or kilometers
steps: Take triangle sides a = 5 m, b = 300 cm, c = 0.04 km
Convert all sides to same unit
Calculate semi-perimeter s
Use Heron’s formula to calculate area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies formula in practical unit-conversion scenarios

activities_projects.title: Area of Triangles from Real-Life Plots
activities_projects.objective: Measure triangular land plots and calculate area using Heron’s formula
steps: Measure three sides of a triangular plot
Calculate semi-perimeter s
Use Heron’s formula to find area
Compare with approximate physical measurement if possible
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula in real-world measurement
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate measurement and area calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Triangle Area Using Multiple Methods
activities_projects.objective: Compare area using Heron’s formula, ½ × base × height, and coordinate method
steps: Draw a triangle and measure sides and height
Calculate area using ½ × base × height
Calculate area using Heron’s formula
If coordinates are known, calculate using determinant formula
Compare all results
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Integrates multiple methods for area calculation
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate calculations using all methods (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Advanced Project: Triangular Pavilion Layout
activities_projects.objective: Design a triangular pavilion with precise area calculation using Heron’s formula
steps: Draw a triangular pavilion layout to scale
Measure or assign side lengths
Calculate semi-perimeter and area using Heron’s formula
Plan placement of interior sections based on area
Label all sides and angles
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula in advanced real-life design project
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct design, measurement, and area calculation (10 marks)
